D3DTILE

Describes a tile. Tiles are GPU views that significantly improve rendering speed for render targets and depth buffers by reorganizing memory to improve bandwidth, and by allowing depth buffers to be compressed.

typedef struct _D3DTILE        // Xbox extension
{                   
    DWORD   Flags;
    void*   pMemory;
    DWORD   Size;
    DWORD   Pitch;
    DWORD   ZStartTag; 
    DWORD   ZOffset;
} D3DTILE;

Members

Flags
This may be the value zero, or one or more of the following flags ORed together. These flags describe the characteristics of the tile.
D3DTILE_FLAGS_ZBUFFER
Tile will be used to hold depth targets.
D3DTILE_FLAGS_ZCOMPRESS
Tile for a z buffer is to be compressed.
D3DTILE_FLAGS_Z32BITS
Gives the depth format for a tiled z buffer. D3DTILE_FLAGS_Z16BITS may not be set at the same time.
D3DTILE_FLAGS_Z16BITS
Gives the depth format for a tiled z buffer. D3DTILE_FLAGS_Z32BITS may not be set at the same time.
pMemory
Pointer to the start of the contiguous memory range that is to be tiled. The memory should allocated using D3D_AllocContiguousMemory with D3DTILE_ALIGNMENT (16k) byte alignment.
Size
Size of the memory that is to be tiled and is pointed to by pMemory. Size should be calculated using a height that is a multiple of 16 (and the result should be rounded up to the next multiple of 16K).
Pitch
Specifies the pitch of the tile. Any surface created in the tiled memory should share this same pitch. The hardware supports only a limited number of pitches, ranging from 0x200 to 0xE000. Only the following pitches are supported:
ConstantPitch Value
D3DTILE_PITCH_0200      0x0200
D3DTILE_PITCH_03000x0300
D3DTILE_PITCH_04000x0400
D3DTILE_PITCH_05000x0500
D3DTILE_PITCH_06000x0600
D3DTILE_PITCH_07000x0700
D3DTILE_PITCH_08000x0800
D3DTILE_PITCH_0A000x0A00
D3DTILE_PITCH_0C000x0C00
D3DTILE_PITCH_0E000x0E00
D3DTILE_PITCH_10000x1000
D3DTILE_PITCH_14000x1400
D3DTILE_PITCH_18000x1800
D3DTILE_PITCH_1C000x1C00
D3DTILE_PITCH_20000x2000
D3DTILE_PITCH_28000x2800
D3DTILE_PITCH_30000x3000
D3DTILE_PITCH_38000x3800
D3DTILE_PITCH_40000x4000
D3DTILE_PITCH_50000x5000
D3DTILE_PITCH_60000x6000
D3DTILE_PITCH_70000x7000
D3DTILE_PITCH_80000x8000
D3DTILE_PITCH_A0000xA000
D3DTILE_PITCH_C0000xC000
D3DTILE_PITCH_E0000xE000
ZStartTag
Z-Compressed tiles require that memory be allocated from special tag RAM, for the size of the depth buffer. Every D3DTILE_TAGSIZE (64) bytes of depth buffer require that one tag bit be allocated from the special tag RAM. There are D3DTILE_MAXTAGS (76800) total tag RAM bits available, and the tag bits can only be allocated in bundles of 256. It is up to the caller to manage the allocations from tag RAM by appropriately setting ZStartTag to the start of a consecutive, unused section of tag RAM large enough to represent the tile. The following bit of code show how you can set the ZStartTag member for a new tile, assuming you have pDevice, a valid pointer to an IDirect3DDevice8 interface. 
D3DTILE lastReservedTile; // tiles 0 and 1 are reserved
D3DTILE newTile;

pDevice->GetTile( 1, &lastReservedTile );
newTile.ZStartTag = D3DTILE_ZENDTAG( &lastReservedTile );

The macro D3DTILE_ZENDTAG has been provided to help to help determine where to start in tag RAM. In this example, it is assumed that newTile is the first tile to be created by the user. Tile 0 and 1 are reserved by Direct3D for the primary render target and primary depth buffer, assuming that these are automatically created.

For automatically created depth buffers (EnableAutoDepthStencil is TRUE), Direct3D always reserves a corresponding range of tags bits, starting at 0. If IDirect3DDevice8::Reset is called, it's the callers responsibility to ensure that there are enough free tag bits starting at 0 to satisfy the new mode.

ZOffset
It's possible to create a compressed tile even if there are not enough tag bits free to entirely cover it, so long as the tags bits are allocated from the very end of the tag RAM buffer. In this case, ZOffset may be used to specify an offset in order to vertically position the tag bits to correspond to the area of the buffer that would make most benefit from the compression. ZOffset is a byte offset from the start of the frame buffer, and must be a multiple of D3DTILE_ALIGNMENT.

This offset applies to only one depth buffer, the one that uses up all available tag RAMs. Because of the way in which tiled memory is organized, the mapping of the offset to screen locations, and likewise the portion of a partially-covered buffer that's actually compressible, are only roughly top-to-bottom.

Requirements

  Header: Declared in d3d8typs.h.

See Also

D3DTILE_ZENDTAG, IDirect3DDevice8::GetTile, IDirect3DDevice8::SetTile
